intro: Siphocampylus pendulifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the "pendulous bellflower." The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Siphocampylus pendulifolius include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
